★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.36 4.52 3.72 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.60 0.79 0.51 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.28 1.00 1.09 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.48 2.73 2.12 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.88 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.22 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.31
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.55
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.68
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Case-Mix Index Ratio

facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-09 20 13 7 88 1 88
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-29 27 14 13 207 1 207
Total weighted health score
117.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (60)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
56 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-12-11 2026-01-05
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-12-11 2026-01-05
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-12-11 2026-01-05
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-12-11 2026-01-05
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2025-12-11 2026-01-05
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-12-11 2026-01-05
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-08-29 2025-09-19
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0678 Provide basic life support, including CPR, prior to the arrival of emergency medical personnel , subject to physician orders and the resident’s advance directives. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-02-09 2025-02-20
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2025-02-09 2025-03-05
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2024-11-06 2024-12-01
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2024-11-06 2024-11-26
0660 Plan the resident's discharge to meet the resident's goals and needs. D 2024-07-18 2024-08-27
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2024-07-18 2024-08-27
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2024-07-18 2024-08-27
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-07-18 2024-08-27
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-06-26 2024-07-19
0624 Prepare residents for a safe transfer or discharge from the nursing home. D 2024-06-26 2024-07-19
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted E 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. E 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. D 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0940 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program for all new and existing staff members. E 2024-01-29 2024-02-25
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-10-27 2023-10-27
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2023-05-16 2023-06-16
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-16
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. H 2023-03-15 2023-04-13
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2023-03-15 2023-04-13
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2021-12-02 2022-02-09
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2021-12-02 2022-02-09
